source: .specs/agent-scenarios.md â†’ Feature: Architect/Editor model pair (instantiation b: planâ†’implement mode-pair, Claude Code plan mode)
notes: Instantiation (b) of architect-editor-pair: a planâ†’implement MODE pair (one model) across the approval gate, distinct from user-blocking-plan-mode-approval (2.18) which terminates at the waiting gate. This spec asserts the FULL arc readyâ†’workingâ†’waitingâ†’workingâ†’ready: the architect/plan phase drives working, ExitPlanMode gates the session into waiting (no intervening ready â€” invariant on plan_gate), the user approves, the editor/implement phase recovers waitingâ†’working and applies a real Edit/Write, and the turn completes â†’ready. The events.jsonl phase DSL carries no token or model field, so two prose-asserted signals (verified at record time, not by the validator) are: SessionMetrics.TotalTokens after turn_end > at plan_gate (tokens accumulate across the plan and implement phases), and ModelName is a single consistent value across both phases (one model â€” the mode-pair instantiation, not the Aider two-model shape). The editor_implement phase is anchored same_session_as architect_plan so the post-approval recovery is proven on the SAME session that gated, not a co-occurring session.

text: Session appears in ready after agent launch (plan mode)
User sends the task in plan mode â†’ readyâ†’working (architect/plan phase begins)

Agent proposes a plan via ExitPlanMode â†’ workingâ†’waiting (plan-approval gate). The 'no ready before approval' invariant is enforced STRUCTURALLY by the phase chain, not a window invariant: plan_gate is the first waiting after architect_plan(working), editor_implement is the first working after plan_gate, and turn_end is the first ready after editor_implement â€” so the ordering workingâ†’waitingâ†’workingâ†’ready itself proves no ready interposed between plan emission and the approval recovery. A 'no state_transition to ready' invariant here is WRONG for a full-arc cell (its window is open-ended with no DurationAtLeastMs, so it would catch the legitimate post-approval turn_end ready); this cell, unlike 2.18, DOES go ready after approval.
User approves the plan â†’ waitingâ†’working (editor/implement phase begins; the agent applies the edits)
Implement turn finishes (real Edit/Write applied; file created) â†’ workingâ†’ready
